steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation for a 45-45-90 triangle
steps.content:
An isosceles right triangle has legs of equal length. Let's call the leg length $a$.

In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse equals $a\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is: $$2a + a\sqrt{2} = a(2 + \sqrt{2}) = 94 + 94\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Match the leg length to the perimeter
steps.content: If $a=47\sqrt{2}$, then the perimeter is $$47\sqrt{2}(2+\sqrt{2})=94\sqrt{2}+94$$ This is the same as the given perimeter $94+94\sqrt{2}$. Therefore the leg length is $47\sqrt{2}$, so B is correct.
